name: Nigeria Data Protection Act, 2023 (NDPA)
summary: Nigeria's first comprehensive data protection statute replaces the earlier Nigeria Data Protection Regulation (NDPR) of 2019. It establishes the Nigeria Data Protection Commission (NDPC) as a standalone regulatory body and provides a legal framework for the processing of personal data by public and private entities.
keyRequirements: Obtain informed, specific, freely given, and unambiguous consent
Execute data processing agreements with all data processors
Conduct data protection impact assessments for high-risk processing
Appoint a Data Protection Officer where required
Register with the NDPC as a data controller of major importance (if applicable)
Implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures
Notify the NDPC of data breaches within 72 hours
penalties: Data controllers of major importance: fines up to NGN 10 million or 2% of annual gross revenue, whichever is higher. Other organizations: fines up to NGN 2 million or 2% of annual gross revenue. Enforced by the Nigeria Data Protection Commission (NDPC).
